title: Ensure end-to-end TLS encryption is enabled

input_row.title: Ensure end-to-end TLS encryption is enabled
input_row.description: End-to-end (E2E) TLS encryption ensures that front-end to worker communication within App Service deployment slots is encrypted using TLS. Without this feature, while incoming HTTPS requests are encrypted to the front ends, the traffic from front ends to workers running the application workloads would travel unencrypted inside Azure’s infrastructure.
input_row.rationale: E2E TLS helps ensure full encryption of traffic between: • Clients and front ends • Front ends and worker processes hosting the application
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, ensure that Enable end-to-end TLS encryption is set to On. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each app and deployment slot. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list apps: az webapp list For each app, run the following command to list deployment slots: az webapp deployment slot list --resource-group --name For each deployment slot, ensure that endToEndEncryptionEnabled is set to true. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 123aed70-491a-4f07-a569-e1f3a8dd651e - Name: 'App Service app slots should enable end to end encryption'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, next to Enable end-to-end TLS encryption, click the radio button next to On. 7. Click Save. 8. Click Continue. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each app and deployment slot requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command to enable end-to-end TLS encryption: az resource update --resource-group --name /slots/ --resource-type "Microsoft.Web/sites" -- set properties.endToEndEncryptionEnabled=true
input_row.impact: Enabling end-to-end TLS encryption may introduce minimal latency and require additional configuration of certificates and HTTPS settings to ensure compatibility.
input_row.default_value: By default, end-to-end TLS encryption is disabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control description emphasizes the importance of end-to-end TLS encryption for secure communication. 
- Since AWS has a related service (Elastic Beanstalk and API Gateway) which can utilize HTTPS/TLS configurations, this can be checked using AWS APIs.
- The audit and remediation sections provide command sequences for managing similar configurations, implying that automatic checks would be practical.
- There are respective API calls and AWS CLI commands to check TLS settings for applications running on AWS services, satisfying the criteria for an automated check.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_elasticbeanstalk_application_end_to_end_tls_encryption_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
- The automated program would primarily check the following:
  - Use the AWS CLI command `aws elasticbeanstalk describe-application-settings --application-name your-app-name` to retrieve the current application settings and verify whether TLS is configured for end-to-end encryption.
  - Alternatively, utilize boto3 SDK methods:
    - `client = boto3.client('elasticbeanstalk')`
    - `response = client.describe_application_settings(ApplicationName='your-app-name')`
      - Verify the attributes related to secure connections and TLS settings from the response.
  - Specifically, the program should analyze `EnvironmentSettings` returned from the API call, looking for properties indicating that TLS is enabled.
  - Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
    - Pass: `endToEndEncryptionEnabled` is set to `true` or equivalent attribute confirming TLS encryption in transit is enabled.
    - Fail: The attribute is not present or is set to `false` indicating that end-to-end encryption is not enabled.

This analysis outlines a practical approach leveraging AWS capabilities to ensure compliance with the specified security standard.
